title: erigeron ovinus
intro: Erigeron ovinus, commonly known as sheep's bit or fleabane, is a perennial plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. Erigeron ovinus is believed to provide health benefits like reducing fever, relieving headaches, and soothing coughs due to its expectorant and diuretic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed internally to harness its medicinal properties.
